pr_title: Local logging to file, replay from logs
pr_body:
Implements #321. Basically visdom would simply dispose of logs that failed, and if a user closed panes on the server there was no recourse to getting them back. This introduces a way to store the logs on the local visdom client's machine as well as the remote visdom server, ensuring the data remains safe. It also allows replaying of the data.

Basic implementation centers around logging the endpoint and json of every plotting event to a file, and then that file can later be replayed with that info. One gotcha is that all plots have to know what the window id was that they were plotted with in order to make sure that recorded updates to those plots will properly update the correct plot. This does mean that logs cannot be replayed multiple times in the same environment.

Demo:
![visdomreplaylog](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1276867/41258722-36fd1c36-6d9f-11e8-9f4a-81b566bad54c.gif)


This PR also includes documentation for some of the Visdom object params

Closes #321

Every commit in every branch gets uploaded to Test PyPI, e.g. https://test.pypi.org/project/httplib2/0.11.1.post1803270210/
Every tagged commit in master gets uploaded to Public PyPI. I did not produce separate tag to test this.

Also this patch includes semi-automation of release process. It helped me a lot with Eventlet.

Release process now: run `script/release` that's all to remember. In interactive (default) mode it will guide through bumping version number, editing `CHANGELOG`, creating release commit and tag, answer `n` to upload to pypi question.

- `script/release` will push new tag to Github
- Github will invoke Travis build hook
- Travis will build and upload PyPI package, granted all tests pass


`script/release` considers these sources of actual version information:
- `python3/httplib2/__init__.py:__version__ = 'foobar'`
- git tag in `-auto` mode (on Travis)
- what user entered in interactive mode

Current implementation of Report Portal `pytest` agent is not compatible with distributed tests execution.
Thus, following workaround has been added to notify end user about this problem.
User will get exception in case when plug-in `xdist` is installed  with output like:
```bash
 $ pytest --rp-launch TestRPIssue29 test_script.py
...
INTERNALERROR> Exception: pytest report portal is not compatible with 'xdist' plugin.
```
If it is required to have this plug-in installed for e.g. `unittest`, this plug-in can be temporary disabled for test which running with Repor Portal integration:
```bash
$ pytest -p no:xdist --rp-launch TestRPIssue29 test_script.py
```

pr_title: Alternative: Work around Basic Authentication for challenge dir
pr_body:
This is an alternative to #5461

Unfortunately, the way that Apache merges the configuration directives is different for mod_rewrite and <Location> / <Directory> directives.

To work around basic auth in VirtualHosts, the challenge override `Include` now has an added `<Location>` block, and is included twice in the `<VirtualHost>`: once in the beginning to tackle `mod_rewrite` issues, and once in the end to fix issues with `<Directory>` and `<Location>`.

pr_body: Fixes #243
Compilestats & General logging output

This PR does a few things. In the first two commits:

- We no longer test on versions of Django and Python which are no longer supported.
- Remove all pre-django 1.8 hacks.

Then, in a separate commit, we remove support for Django 1.8 as well. I kept this separate because I may understand the argument to keep Django 1.8 as it is still supported upstream... *however*, Django 1.11 is the new LTS and, given the complexity of django-polymorphic and the amount of hacks dropping 1.8 removes, I would *very highly* recommend dropping 1.8 support as well, and recommend django-polymorphic==1.2 to Django 1.8 users.

As a followup to this, I am also improving compatibility with Django 2.0, to the point that tests now pass on django-dev. I cannot vouch for their reliability, however, especially given that I still see the following warning on 1.11 with -Wall:

pr_title: WIP: add option to upgrade pip/setuptools inside venv
pr_body:
Fixes https://github.com/tox-dev/tox-venv/issues/17

This PR adds a `--upgrade-venv` command line option as well as a `upgrade_venv` config (boolean) option, that is false by default. When True, the pip and setuptools inside the test venvs are upgraded to the currently available version on PyPI.

This is to make the behavior of the environments created via tox-venv more similar to the default ones created with virtualenv, where the default behavior is to always install the latest pip and setuptools (and wheel), unless --no-dowload option is passed.

I haven't written a specific test yet because I first would like to gather some feedback that this is the way to do it, as I've never written a tox plugin myself and am not familiar with tox API.

Changes proposed in this pull request:


@WilliamHPNielsen

- Moved display mode above scale in image tile options
- Scale renamed to resolution
- Rename some of the display modes, provide brief explanations of each one
- [Fixed a bug](https://github.com/castlecms/castle.cms/commit/2397772ea9c7273541495582be5307904981a98c) that occurs when resolution is set to original and display is set to one of the cropping modes

This PR adds 3 features:
1. Resumable resizing. If a previous resize operation failed or if more images were added to the dataset, `resume=True` (the new default) will find and resize them. The previous behavior would not attempt to perform any resizing if the `targ` directory existed. By checking all files at the beginning of the run, it's comparable in speed to the original (checking if files exist individually is significantly slower).
2. Add support for custom resizing functions via `fn` parameter. (default=None which defaults to im.resize)
3. Add error handling to image resizing -- rather than crashing mid run, any errors are output at the end

~I also added mypy types to the functions that I touched.~ I'm happy to remove if they're bothersome, but they make development easier in IDEs like PyCharm as well as making the code a bit easier to use in the future.
Fixes #678

pr_title: Allow for access of final progress stats
pr_body:
This refactors some of the logic around the processing of progress
packets so that the final (sum) of progress counters is accessible. It
includes the following changes:

- make QueryResult know how to process and `store()` its own packets.
- Make ProgressQueryResult class a subclass of QueryResult.
- ProgressQueryResult can process progress packets.
- Less encapsulation breakage (Client reaching into QueryResult objects)
- Client now provides generator of packets to QueryResult to iterate on
- ProgressQueryResult now aggregates bytes as well as rows (although
bytes are not returned in the generator to preserve the current
interface)
- Final sum of progress counters is accessible as `progress_totals`

This PR fixes both https://github.com/python-security/pyt/issues/1 and https://github.com/python-security/pyt/issues/26 as well as just adds a comment here our there and fixes one file I missed with regard to import order.

Let me know what you think.

Cheers.

### Type

- [X] Bugfix
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] New feature

### Description of the changes

Per discussion with @Tobotimus in discord, an alt to #1980 , #2006 , #2007 since deprecating the current usage shouldn't be necessary. 

This reverts the hotfix #2004 , and refactors `get_messages_for_deletion`. This also adds a note in the doc string about the unsafe usage case no core code uses, and how to handle that case should we care about it in the future, rather than apply any special casing here.

### Type

- Bugfix

### Description of the changes
Following Rapptz/discord.py@f8f8f41, some things broke. This PR should fix those things.

### Type

- [X] Bugfix
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] New feature

### Description of the changes

This begins a more long-term fix. Part of that is changing a function signature, and as such, steps are being taken to do this with a proper warning for 3rd party devs using this utility function.

This is targeting beta 20, but promises changes in beta 21, and beta 22 
PRs will be opened and connected to this.

### Type

- [x] Bugfix
- [ ] Enhancement
- [ ] New feature

### Description of the changes

Fixes #1926 by returning False when import of parent package fails.

### Type

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] Enhancement
- [ ] New feature

### Description of the changes
#1367 

This adds in an update check that is done:
- on startup (if out of date, this is stated when the startup screen is printed)
- in `[p]info` (via a field in the embed)

ATM this doesn't DM the bot owner, though that could certainly be added in if desired

pr_title: [V3 Docs] Fixed incorrect link in description in Docs
pr_body:
This fixes the bug I reported here: https://github.com/Cog-Creators/Red-DiscordBot/issues/1091

(Tested locally in sphinx — I didn’t pray, Kowlin ;)

### Type

- [ ] Bugfix
- [x] Enhancement
- [ ] New feature

### Description of the changes
This adds a couple of things to [p]userinfo
- Display of previous names and nicknames (if there are any tracked)
- Display of the user's current voice channel (if they are in one)

The logic that gets and escapes the names and nicknames was moved out of `[p]names` to its own function to be usable by more than just `[p]names`. `[p]userinfo` was also moved to `Mod` as a result of this (as opposed to using `bot.get_cog` just to be able to get previous names/nicks, which are stored in Mod's config)

Have already tested this as well

This creates a wrapper console script which allows behave2cucumber to be called directly from the commandline without having to invoke "python -m"

Note, the main function should be standalone e.g. not depend on any global level input (to avoid any issues).
so the main function should just be:

if __name__ == '__main__':
     main()

and any sys.argv input checking should happen inside of main()

* Fix to initialize the `requests` connection once per Goose object
* Additional parameters to allow for proxies, authorization, and headers for the `requests` connection
* Update the `known_context_patterns` property handling to hopefully make it easier to add multiple new patterns

* Add base64 image parsing
   * including test
* Resolves #7

(`2957 > <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2957>`_) >- Fix ``ZeroDivisionError`` when using the ``testmon`` plugin when no tests > were actually collected. (`2984 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2984>`_) >- The ``pytest-capturelog`` plugin is now also blacklisted, avoiding errors when > running pytest with it still installed. Those Python versions > are EOL for some time now and incur maintenance and compatibility costs on > the pytest core team, and following up with the rest of the community we > decided that they will no longer be supported starting on this version. Users > which still require those versions should pin pytest to ``<3.3``. (`2812 > <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2812>`_) >- Remove internal ``_preloadplugins()`` function. This removal is part of the > ``pytest_namespace()`` hook deprecation. (`2636 > <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2636>`_) >- Internally change ``CallSpec2`` to have a list of marks instead of a broken > mapping of keywords. This removes the keywords attribute of the internal > ``CallSpec2`` class. (`2672 > <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2672>`_) >- Remove ParameterSet.deprecated_arg_dict - its not a public api and the lack > of the underscore was a naming error. (`2675 > <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2675>`_) >- Remove the internal multi-typed attribute ``Node._evalskip`` and replace it > with the boolean ``Node._skipped_by_mark``. (`2767 > <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2767>`_) >- The ``params`` list passed to ``pytest.fixture`` is now for > all effects considered immutable and frozen at the moment of the ``pytest.fixture`` > call. Previously the list could be changed before the first invocation of the fixture > allowing for a form of dynamic parametrization (for example, updated from command-line options), > but this was an unwanted implementation detail which complicated the internals and prevented > some internal cleanup. See issue `2959 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2959>`_ > for details and a recommended workaround. >Features >-------- >- ``pytest_fixture_post_finalizer`` hook can now receive a ``request`` > argument. Also, a ``caplog`` fixture is available that enables users to test > the captured log during specific tests (similar to ``capsys`` for example). > For more information, please see the `logging docs > <https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/logging.html>`_. People with a "+" by their >names contributed a patch for the first time. >* Allan Haldane >* Brandon Carter >* Charles Harris >* Eric Wieser >* Iryna Shcherbina + >* James Bourbeau + >* Jonathan Helmus >* Julian Taylor >* Matti Picus >* Michael Lamparski + >* Michael Seifert >* Ralf Gommers >Pull requests merged >==================== >A total of 22 pull requests were merged for this release. >* 9390 BUG: Return the poly1d coefficients array directly >* 9555 BUG: Fix regression in 1.13.x in distutils.mingw32ccompiler. >* 9556 BUG: Fix true_divide when dtype=np.float64 specified. >* 9557 DOC: Fix some rst markup in numpy/doc/basics.py. >* 9558 BLD: Remove -xhost flag from IntelFCompiler. >* 9559 DOC: Removes broken docstring example (source code, png, pdf)... >* 9580 BUG: Add hypot and cabs functions to WIN32 blacklist. >* 9732 BUG: Make scalar function elision check if temp is writeable. >* 9736 BUG: Various fixes to np.gradient >* 9742 BUG: Fix np.pad for CVE-2017-12852 >* 9744 BUG: Check for exception in sort functions, add tests >* 9745 DOC: Add whitespace after "versionadded::" directive so it actually... >* 9746 BUG: Memory leak in np.dot of size 0 >* 9747 BUG: Adjust gfortran version search regex >* 9757 BUG: Cython 0.27 breaks NumPy on Python 3. >* 9764 BUG: Ensure `_npy_scaled_cexp{,f,l}` is defined when needed. >* 9765 BUG: PyArray_CountNonzero does not check for exceptions >* 9766 BUG: Fixes histogram monotonicity check for unsigned bin values >* 9767 BUG: Ensure consistent result dtype of count_nonzero >* 9771 BUG: MAINT: Fix mtrand for Cython 0.27. >* 9772 DOC: Create the 1.13.2 release notes. >* 9794 DOC: Create 1.13.3 release notes. >========================== That's it for now! The only difference is that I have retained the additional data from v1.0.0 in the Miller-et-al directory which was not present in my development version.

It would be good to get this released as FAIR as v1.1.0 which is as described in https://www.geosci-model-dev-discuss.net/gmd-2017-266/ . The code has been requested to be released, which is strongly encouraged for GMD model description papers.

Zeb, I hope you don't mind that you weren't included as an author on the paper. It seems that our PIs decided to work on FAIR independently - I wrote an entire model in parallel based on a very early version, found out that FAIR v1.0 existed already, and then had to rewrite everything to ensure back-compatibility!

pr_title: Allowing config to have humans after bots. Fixes #175.
pr_body:
Human index is supposed to be 0 for the first human, 1 for the second human, etc, without counting bots. Player index includes bots, therefore passing it directly into the human index field was incorrect.

I made a little class to help us keep track of the human index across multiple calls to load_bot_config.

This PR also includes an updated core dll with the following fixes:
- We were attempting to sort player configs so that humans spawned first, but the sort comparator had a serious bug that stopped it from working properly
- Once I fixed the sorting, I found that the mechanism to map the original player index to the sorted game objects was never fully implemented. Hidden by the sorting bug.
- Once I fixed the mapping, I found that our game data reader never utilized the mapping, so I fixed that.

After this fix, you can now configure a human or party_member_bot at any index you like.

pr_body:
This should fix the problem on Unix systems like Mac or Linux.

I had the problem to run a program as a background daemon detached from an active login shell. I now use the effective user id get the username.
